text:
Summary:
- DoD-facing contact center operator supporting critical comms (emergency dispatch, infra ops).
- Must deploy in completely air-gapped on-prem enclave maintained by customer SOC. No egress to public internet allowed; outbound updates require packaged media and manual approval.
- Primary features wanted: agent assist (real-time suggestions + canned responses), ticket summarization (post-call TL;DR), tool-calling integration (RA/TA to call internal CRM/dispatch tools), offline reranking for search across document stores, and automated playbook triggers for high-severity incidents.

Key constraints/requirements:
- Security: FIPS 140-2 HSM required for key management, SOC2+ISO27001 posture, detailed audit logs retained 7+ years, SIEM integration (Splunk), SSO via SAML and RBAC.
- Compliance: anticipating IL4/DoD STIG checks; vendor to provide SBOM, signed supply chain attestation...

title: DayStarter Labs

Observed good latency on small prompts; ran into rate limit errors during parallel test harness on 2026-03-13. No AE t...
text:
Inbound PLG signup. Solo founder (iOS dev) experimenting with chat + semantic search for a tiny customer service automation in-app. Created account 2026-03-01, used free credits to run prompt tests. Observed good latency on small prompts; ran into rate limit errors during parallel test harness on 2026-03-13. No AE touch yet — flagged as inbound-growth. Wants hosted API only (public cloud).

Shorthand from signup form: "early prototype, budget tiny, need quick integration into iOS app, prefer predictable per-token pricing, no enterprise SSO now".

Key quotes from in-app feedback form: "I need embeddings + chat that just work — low ops, low cost. If the demo shows <100ms p95 for short prompts I'm sold."

Asks: sample iOS SDK snippet, client-side key rotation guidance, basic cost calc for 100k tokens/mo. Interested in benching Redwood-hosted model vs third-party hosted endpoints (cost/la...

title: Bridgepoint Infer Works
preview: Status: mid-market product org evaluating Redwood. Short background: Bridgepoint builds a personalization and support layer that uses embeddings + reranking to power the in-product help center and recommended content. Started on Hosted API to validate latency/cost; moving to Dedicated for predictable throughput and ...
text:
Status: mid-market product org evaluating Redwood. Short background: Bridgepoint builds a personalization and support layer that uses embeddings + reranking to power the in-product help center and recommended content. Started on Hosted API to validate latency/cost; moving to Dedicated for predictable throughput and data isolation.

POC summary: 14-day hosted POC completed. Key metrics: avg token cost (hosted) higher than target; latency within SLO for short chats (median 60ms) but occasional tail spikes at 95th pctl ~110-130ms. SE tuning (Priya) improved batching and cache hit rate to 42%. They want Dedicated for lower unit cost and better isolation.

Pricing/packaging discussion notes (primary focus):
- Bridgepoint wants 3-year preferred pricing but finance hesitant; leaning toward 12-24 month commit.
- Primary ask: commit-level to cover baseline 50qps equivalent (est. sustained thro...

title: Nebula Coder Collective
preview: Small devtool startup building an in-IDE assistant + cross-repo search. Inbound via self-serve, very hands-on dev founders (2 engineers + 1 PM). Primary technical asks: reliable streaming API with reconnect semantics for editor sessions, support for 32k+ effective context (prefer 64k eventually), and client-side rat...
text: Small devtool startup building an in-IDE assistant + cross-repo search. Inbound via self-serve, very hands-on dev founders (2 engineers + 1 PM). Primary technical asks: reliable streaming API with reconnect semantics for editor sessions, support for 32k+ effective context (prefer 64k eventually), and client-side rate limit headers to implement graceful backoff. They do whole-file + dependency-context for completions (many small files concatenated). Current provider returns 429s on spikes; they want soft-burst handling or short-term credits. Cost is a gating factor; POC monthly budget <$1.5k. Want simple Node/TS SDK snippets that show streaming chunks and how to resume with last token-id. Also asked about token-level billing visibility in dashboard and per-route usage limits. Security: audit logs and team API key rotation OK for now; will ask for SOC2 in future. CTO comment: "If we can...

title: Northstar Customer AI
preview:

text:
Quick notes from meetings and threads:
- Company: mid-market SaaS (helpdesk focused), productized chat agent for multilingual support.
- Primary ask: hosted API first to validate performance, then Dedicated if predictable latency/cost needed.
- POC scope: streaming (server->client) + tool calling (order status, account lookup via webhook) + safety filters.
- Latency SLA: target first-token <1.5s for US region (quoted repeatedly by PM: "first token must feel instant for chat"). Tail and p95 also important.
- Throughput estimate: ~200-400 concurrent active chats during peaks; expected 1M tokens/mo initial, growth to 3-5M. Cost sensitive but will pay for predictable performance.
- Model preferences: open to Llama2/Redwood-verified variants + possibility of smaller quantized models for routing cheaper requests. Interested in fast streaming SDK and function-calling compatibility.
- Routing...

title: Novaforge Product Systems
preview: Mid-market product analytics vendor. Primary workloads: low-latency chat support for end-users (SLO p95 < 150ms), high-throughput embeddings for semantic search and reranking (target 80–120 reqs/s), and nightly batch rerank for freshness (latency tolerant). Cost sensitivity high: target cost per 100k tokens must be ...
text: Mid-market product analytics vendor. Primary workloads: low-latency chat support for end-users (SLO p95 < 150ms), high-throughput embeddings for semantic search and reranking (target 80–120 reqs/s), and nightly batch rerank for freshness (latency tolerant). Cost sensitivity high: target cost per 100k tokens must be < $X (Proprietary threshold discussed in call). Need ability to route EU traffic to Dedicated region due to data residency. Must have SSO/SAML, audit logs, and KMS integration. Quantization tolerances: cannot degrade top-3 accuracy on rerank eval by >1.5% — must validate via Redwood eval harness. Preferred deployment path: start Hosted API for fast time-to-value -> migrate to Dedicated reserved capacity when unit economics validated -> hybrid routing for EU customers.

title: BriefSpark Launchpad
preview:

CRM-style summary: brief, pragmatic SMB — 25 employees, US sales org, high call volume (~500 calls/mo across team). Currently uses in-house Node app to stitch Zoom transcriptions; manual redaction and QA steps take ~6-8 hours/wk. G...
text:
Lead source: inbound from app partner (sales enablement/call-summaries marketplace).

CRM-style summary: brief, pragmatic SMB — 25 employees, US sales org, high call volume (~500 calls/mo across team). Currently uses in-house Node app to stitch Zoom transcriptions; manual redaction and QA steps take ~6-8 hours/wk. Goal: reduce manual post-call work; auto-generate 3-5 bullet summaries + action items; flag PII.

PII & retention concerns (customer quotes):
- 'We can't have raw transcripts stored for more than 7 days.'
- 'Need a simple way to redact or avoid storing email/cc numbers in records.'
- 'If we use hosted API, can we ensure transcripts are ephemeral and not used for model training?'

What they care about: latency (wants <1.5s/token for streaming summary), pricing predictability (they run small but frequent jobs), simple SDK and drop-in integration for Node, minimal infra overhea...

text:
Inbound from self-serve signup — product team (CTO Ari K.) signed up for trial on 2026-03-02. Wants hosted API for production pilot (customer support bot + product search embeddings).

Key points from discovery call (2026-03-09 with Jordan + Maya):
- Use-case: chat-based support for mid-ticket inquiries (order status, returns), and embeddings for site search + facet-aware rerank.
- Latency need: p95 < 200ms for chat; search latency target < 120ms.
- Throughput: small initial footprint — ~30 qps peak for search, ~5 concurrent chat sessions; expects growth to ~200 qps in 6–9 months.
- Model preference: open-ish models (cost-aware) but wants Redwood-verified performance notes; fine with a mid-sized LLaMA/Mistral variant if latency/cost tradeoff is good.
- Security: SOC2 required; SSO (SAML) for internal console access; audit logs and data retention controls are a gating item for procurem...

text:
Inbound from webinar signup (Mar 2). Founder/CTO: Maya Ortiz (quote: 'we need cheap tokens at scale — think 3–5M tokens/day initially').

Discovery call 2026-03-03 (Jordan + Priya). Key points:
- Small engineering team (mobile + CMS integrations).
- Primary workload: high-throughput marketing content (email subject lines, ad variants, short blog drafts).
- Cost sensitive: wants to compare hosted cost vs self-host + open models. Leaning hosted for faster time-to-value.
- Interested in batching + prefix/KV caching for repeated templates to cut token spend. Asked about automatic caching suggestions.
- Latency target: median <200ms for short-turn generation (<=256 tokens). Will accept slightly higher for long-form.
- Model preferences: open/efficient models preferred (quantized variants ok). Not tied to GPT-family. Curious about Redwood model catalog and verified perf stats.
- Security: c...

text:
Stage notes (summary):
- Mid-market SaaS (customer support product) evaluating Redwood. Started with Hosted API prototyping in Jan — now moving to Dedicated with VPC for production.
- Primary workloads: chat-based support assistant (short-turn multi-turn convos), embeddings for internal doc search + reranking for article suggestions.
- Latency/throughput targets: p95 <150ms for single-turn customer replies, sustained throughput ~150-250 reqs/s during peak; cost sensitivity = medium (focused on predictable committed pricing).
- Model preferences: open-models preferred but need Redwood-verified perf profiles; want automatic fallback routing to smaller, cheaper variants during spikes.
- Security asks: SOC2 Type II evidence, SSO/SAML integration (Okta), audit logging with per-token metadata, retention controls configurable by route, KMS integration for key separation, EU data residency op...

title: CodeQuiver Labs
preview: Lead: small devtools startup building AI code assistant that indexes user repos and provides multi-file suggestions. Main asks: 1) reliable long-context (40k+ tokens) behavior and cost estimates; 2) clear rate-limit semantics and graceful retry patterns; 3) mature SDKs (TS/Go/Python) with streaming + reconnect examp...
text: Lead: small devtools startup building AI code assistant that indexes user repos and provides multi-file suggestions. Main asks: 1) reliable long-context (40k+ tokens) behavior and cost estimates; 2) clear rate-limit semantics and graceful retry patterns; 3) mature SDKs (TS/Go/Python) with streaming + reconnect examples. Tech stack: Node backend, Rust worker for indexing, Postgres. Current approach: OpenAI + self-hosted embedding store; testing Redwood on sandbox for latency/cost comparisons. Quote from CTO: "We need consistent low-latency completions for live coding sessions — 200ms token latency target falling back to cheaper model when needed." Security: wants SOC2 + audit logs; SSO likely later. Notes shorthand: POC scope = 2wk sandbox -> measure token cost, latency profile, rate-limit impact under 5 concurrent sessions. Action items: provide sample SDK snippet that shows streaming...

text:
Lead inbound via blog CTA (payments latency post). Founder CTO (Elena Park) on call 2026-03-01. Wants quick POC on hosted API.
- Primary ask: SOC2 scope & timeline + SSO (SAML) integration checklist.
- "We'd like to start on hosted, but want assurance we can lift-and-shift to VPC in 6-12 months" — CTO.
- Latency target: p95 < 200ms for chat; bursts of 50rps during peak.
- Cost sensitivity: tight; prefers batching/embeddings only where possible.
- Models: open model preference (llama-variants ok); willing to evaluate Redwood-curated small / quantized models.
- Legal wants data retention defaults and audit log export sample.
- POC scope proposed: chat support demo + embeddings for knowledge base search (2-week POC, up to 25k tokens/day).
- Internal stakeholders: CTO Elena Park, Head of Engineering Marcus Liu, Dev lead Priya Shah.
- Notes copied to drive: pricing deck + security-faq link...

text:
- Vertical: enterprise SaaS KB for support teams. Moderate index size (1-1.5M docs ingest). High QPS expectation: target 300-600 qps steady-state for web-facing search.
- Primary ask: embeddings + reranking pipeline; low tail latency for top-K results (p95 < 200ms desired), keep cost-per-query < $0.005 if possible.
- Prefer hosted API (fast time-to-value) but want clear path to VPC/private later.
- Models: prefer small+fast embedding model (128-256 dim) + cross-encoder reranker for top-50 re-rank. Interested in Redwood "Optimize" suggestions (batching, cache, quantization).
- Security: SOC2 required, SSO/SAML mandatory, audit logs + KMS for encryption at rest. Data residency for EU accounts is a future requirement.
- Quote from CTO on call: "We need search that feels instant for customers — 150ms or bust for most queries. Cost needs to scale predictably."
- POC plan: index 1.2M docs (...

title: LatchKey SaaS Insights
preview:

Trial highlights (hosted API):

text:
Account intro: product org (2 PMs) + platform (4 infra) + support ops. Trial started 2026-02-01 using Hosted API (free credits). Primary goal: in-app assistant for product discovery + reduce support triage time.

Trial highlights (hosted API):
- 2-week proof-of-concept: integrated chat endpoint into staging mobile/web clients.
- Collected real traffic sample: 17K requests, ~8.9M tokens total.
- Token mix: ~65% short prompts (<120 prompt tokens) with ~40-60 token completions. 35% long prompts (contextual product history) avg 600 prompt tokens, completions avg 180 tokens.
- Avg prompt tokens per req: 280 (weighted); avg completion tokens: 85.
- Observed p95 latency (hosted): 430ms for short requests, 1.4s for long-context requests (they expect to lower with dedicated).
- Concurrency observed during peak trial: sustained ~40 concurrent requests, burst up to 95 QPS for short bursts.
- Cos...

text:
Account overview: LichenRidge is a mid-market B2B SaaS analytics vendor (product analytics, in-app guidance). High growth; strong enterprise pipeline.

Engagement summary:

- Feb 02: Hosted API POC started (conversation agent + semantic search). SE ran baseline perf tests (see drive link).
- Feb 11: Fireflies demo call ff_2026-02-11_lichenridge_demo_83a1 (recording). CTO: "need predictable costs for scale, can't have big spikes in month 1".
- Feb 25: POC results: average 95th perc latency 210ms for chat (prompt+context 1.2k tokens), embeddings QPS ~140, token cost projection 220k tokens/day baseline.
- Mar 03: Discussion moved to Dedicated for predictability. LichenRidge requested commit-based prici...

text:
Lead source: inbound from marketing webinar + signup via content templates landing page.

Snapshot: small-mid SaaS maker of social/post scheduling + automated blog generation. Mostly self-serve users today. Looking for low-friction hosted API to reduce latency in their generation pipeline and cut per-item cost. High volume use: expect 2-5M tokens/month in steady state; peak campaigns ~10M tokens. Very cost sensitive — primary evaluation lens is $/post and $/embedding.

Workload specifics (from call 2026-02-15):
- Primary: bulk content generation (template-driven blog + social posts). Typical flow: template + user data -> batch generate 50-200 posts per job.
- Secondary: embeddings for semantic search across customer content (monthly reindexing windows).
- Not looking for heavy chat functionality; stateless generation is key.
- Latency target: 300-800ms per generation for single quick ...

text: Lead: Head of Search/Product at PulseForge (Evelyn Park). Mid-market B2B SaaS in product discovery/insights. Wants to replace hybrid TF-IDF+light ML reranker with a dedicated reranking endpoint. Primary ask: reranking endpoint that accepts candidate lists + metadata, supports offline scoring/mini-batch evaluation, can run long tail rerank models with low per-request overhead. Quote from Evelyn: "We need stable p95 latency <180ms for first-pass rerank, and offline reproducible scoring for monthly experiments." Cost sensitivity: willing to pay for Dedicated if unit economics clear. Preference to start on Hosted API to validate quality/latency, then transition to Dedicated for isolation and throughput guarantees. Interested in Redwood Optimize recommendations (quantization suggestions) to reduce GPU costs. Concerns: audit log retention, SSO integration timeline, and contractual data resi...

title: ClearPoint PayTech
preview: Inbound from seed-stage payments startup. Found us via developer blog + community examples. Started with free credits; test integration looks good. Early security asks: "SOC2 in-scope?" and SSO/SAML support. CTO (Mina Patel) is pragmatic — wants hosted first for speed but asks about VPC/on-prem path if scale/regulat...
text: Inbound from seed-stage payments startup. Found us via developer blog + community examples. Started with free credits; test integration looks good. Early security asks: "SOC2 in-scope?" and SSO/SAML support. CTO (Mina Patel) is pragmatic — wants hosted first for speed but asks about VPC/on-prem path if scale/regulatory requires it. Cost sensitivity: expects bursty chat traffic (~100 reqs/min peak) and heavy embeddings for transaction doc search. Latency target: <150ms p99 for chat; embeddings queries <=50ms desired but flexible. Preference: open models + Redwood's curated model catalog; willing to try Redwood-hosted small LLMs for cost control.

text:
Account snapshot:
- Primary contact: Elena Marquez (VP Ops). Support lead: Tom Reed (Head of CX).
- Core problem: automate refund and escalation flows while keeping full audit trail & minimal human touch. Tool-calls must integrate with Zendesk (tickets), internal Refunds API, and Stripe (refund initiation).
- Security posture: SOC2 in progress; strict PCI constraints — "must not persist PANs". Want KMS for keys, audit_logging for all tool-calls, SSO + SCIM for users. Data residency: US.
- Performance targets: p95 reply time for short agent-assist responses <= 350ms; sustained throughput ~700 req/s with safe burst to 2500 req/s during promotions (Black Friday). Cost sensitivity: medium-high — want optimize recommendations + batching to reduce token spend.
- Tool-calling specifics observed in discovery:
 * Need idempotent refund calls (empotency-key) and 2-step confirmation for refunds ...

Needs sub-50ms streaming token latency for inline suggestions; users expect immediate character-by-character streaming. Current prototype uses OpenAI stream but hitting rate limits and jitter. text:
Top-line: building a VS Code copilot extension (Electron + webview) targeted at SMB developer teams. Needs sub-50ms streaming token latency for inline suggestions; users expect immediate character-by-character streaming. Current prototype uses OpenAI stream but hitting rate limits and jitter. Want model pinning per-deployment (dev/demo/prod) and ability to hot-swap model versions without redeploying extension.

Key quotes: "We need streaming to feel native — no 300ms stalls when you type." — CTO Maya Chen.

Auth: plan to use OAuth + short-lived ephemeral keys issued by our backend. Concern: Redwood's rate-limiting semantics and per-user quotas; need guidance on token refresh and graceful fallback when hitting limits.

Data flow & compliance: extension sends small context window + workspace files hashed; embeddings for code search stored in our AWS account. Require SOC2, SSO (SAML), au...

text: Lead: early-stage B2B SaaS (in-app assistants). CTO (Evan Park) very latency-focused: 'streaming is a must — we can't buffer whole responses'. Primary app: helpdesk chat + contextual prompts inside their web app. Target SLOs: first-token under 150ms median, end-to-end reply 300-500ms for short replies. Throughput: peak ~20 rps for US region, normal 3-7 rps. Currently prototyping with open models on public cloud; came in via blog + free credits. Wants simple SDK + streaming websocket support, token-level cost transparency, and easy migration path to Dedicated later if growth requires. Security: SOC2 baseline enough for now, want audit logs and token encryption (KMS) as optional. Questions raised: 1) exact batching behavior with streaming? 2) fallback policy for model throttling/latency spikes? 3) example token-cost for 2k monthly active users w/ avg 120 tokens per conversation. Notes/s...

title: DevStream.io
preview:
Summary: DevStream.io builds an IDE copilot product (VSCode/JetBrains) aimed at professional developer workflows. Main ask: enable ultra-low-latency streaming for inline completions and preserve smooth typing UX when network/compute contention occurs.

POC observations (shorthand):

text:
Summary: DevStream.io builds an IDE copilot product (VSCode/JetBrains) aimed at professional developer workflows. Main ask: enable ultra-low-latency streaming for inline completions and preserve smooth typing UX when network/compute contention occurs.

POC observations (shorthand):
- baseline streaming via hosted API: median TTFT ~120ms, visible jitter when multiple tabs/clients open.
- POC on small dedicated cluster: median TTFT ~45ms, but occasional spikes (100-200ms) when garbage collection/quantized model page-ins happen.
- Inline UX expectations: suggestions should begin streaming within keystroke latency budget (developer perceived TTFT ~ <50ms).
- Token budget tuning: want per-dev soft cap + backpressure semantics (degrade to smaller snippets, not hard failures). Example: 20-token quick completions inline; full-block completions use >100 token budget with explicit user gesture....

text:
Quick notes / CRM style: inbound lead via self-serve trial. They built small POC for product recommendations + chat snippets inside merchant admin. Trial behavior: created key 2026-01-20, executed 1 test request with embeddings + prompt, saw good latency ~120ms per token (subjective), then dropped to zero usage.

What they told us: 'integration was fast but pricing is unclear for bursts' (founder A. Castillo). Also flagged rate-limit 429s on second call — turned out to be misconfigured client retry logic; SE fixed sample code but customer hasn't re-run.

Why at-risk: SMB budget-constrained, competitor offering free credits + tighter billing story; internal PM is pushing opt-out unless we show clear cost savings. No procurement or legal review required for hosted API, but they want SOC2 evidence (we provided doc link).

Committed actions: offered $500 credit + 1-week extension via Jord...

title: Helmshore SupportGrid
preview: Summary: Helmshore is a mid-market SaaS helpdesk provider building an AI-augmented agent and KB search feature (branded 'AnswerGrid'). They ingest product docs, release notes, and agent transcripts. Primary interest: embeddings + reranking pipeline to improve precision on short support queries and reduce false posit...
text:
Summary: Helmshore is a mid-market SaaS helpdesk provider building an AI-augmented agent and KB search feature (branded 'AnswerGrid'). They ingest product docs, release notes, and agent transcripts. Primary interest: embeddings + reranking pipeline to improve precision on short support queries and reduce false positives.

Technical profile / constraints:
- Indexing scope: moderate ~200k canonical docs + frequent delta updates (daily), expect 5-10k new docs/month.
- QPS profile: high sustained QPS for search API; target peaks 150-250 qps during business hours, baseline ~40 qps.
- Latency SLO: 95th < 120ms, 99th < 250ms for reranked top-3 results. Emphasis on tail latency.
- Cost sensitivity: medium — careful about per-query token and embedding cost; target cost per customer query < $0.003 (embeddings + model rerank).
- Model preferences: prefer open/efficient encoders (1536-d embedding...

title: Litigantix Compliance & Discovery

Use-case: replace slow keyword-only search with vector-powered RAG for investigations and compliance reviews. They ingest mixed doc types (email, PDFs, TIFF OCR) ~50M docs; expect steady-state query volume 10k/day with ...
text:
Account: Litigantix (enterprise legal discovery provider). Primary contact: CTO Sarah Villanueva. Use-case: replace slow keyword-only search with vector-powered RAG for investigations and compliance reviews. They ingest mixed doc types (email, PDFs, TIFF OCR) ~50M docs; expect steady-state query volume 10k/day with bursts to 50k/day during litigation windows. Key metrics they care about: index build time, incremental update latency, embedding cardinality and storage, rerank latency (target p95 < 450ms), cost per query target <$0.02 (including embedding + rerank).

Deployment constraints: must run in customer VPC with private control plane; optional on-prem indexer appliance for high-sensitivity clients. KMS/HSM for key management. Retention + audit: 7+ year audit log retention, immutable retention windows, detailed per-request audit trails. Legal insisted on tenant isolation and abili...

preview:

PM quote: 'We need a plan that lets us scale quickly in Q3 without surprising bills.'

SE notes: POC showed consistent latencies; need to validate KV cache reuse across sessions for chat context costs.

Pricing n...
text:
AE notes: Good fit — product team wants predictable costs, clear upgrade path from Hosted -> Dedicated.

PM quote: 'We need a plan that lets us scale quickly in Q3 without surprising bills.'

SE notes: POC showed consistent latencies; need to validate KV cache reuse across sessions for chat context costs.

Pricing notes: Redwood proposal A = 12mo commit, $18k/mo committed, burst up to 3x for 72h windows, overage $0.0009/token; Proposal B = 6mo trial at $10k/mo with higher overage $0.0015/token.

Customer counter: prefers 9mo minimum, wants ramp schedule with credits first 3 months (50% usage credit month1, 25% month2), and hard overage cap at 15% of committed spend per month.

Finance ask: monthly invoicing, ability to convert to quarterly prepay at discounted rate; need clear cost model per 1k tokens for budgeting.

Security: require SSO/SAML, AWS KMS integration, audit logs retained...

title: Arclytic Labs
preview:
POC goal: deterministic, reproducible code completions inside Arclytic IDE plugin with safe progressive rollouts.

- Background: Arclytic builds an in-IDE copiloting product used by internal dev teams + ~80 beta customers. Concern: unpredictable model updates changed completion rank/order and caused flaky build scri...
text:
POC goal: deterministic, reproducible code completions inside Arclytic IDE plugin with safe progressive rollouts.

- Background: Arclytic builds an in-IDE copiloting product used by internal dev teams + ~80 beta customers. Concern: unpredictable model updates changed completion rank/order and caused flaky build scripts. Wants model/version pinning + rollbacks.
- Key asks (from PM & platform lead): pin model version per release channel, reproducible sampling (seed), ability to run canaries split by user cohort, automated eval gates before each rollout.
- Acceptance metrics they care about:
 • compile-failure delta <= 0.2% vs baseline (reject if higher)
 • 95th pctl latency target < 120ms for inline completions (low-latency streaming is critical)
 • token-level quality regression threshold: no more than 3% BLEU/EDR drop on curated prompt set
 • rollback if user-reported error rate > 0.5...

title: Copperleaf Realtime Intelligence
preview: Primary workload: reranking and real-time decisioning for programmatic bids. Targets: sustained 5k rps with burst to 15k rps; p50 < 5ms, p95 < 20ms, p99 < 50ms end-to-end for model decision call. Cost sensitivity: target cost per decision <$0.0006. Model preferences: open weights (Llama2-13B / Mistral-family) with f...
text: Primary workload: reranking and real-time decisioning for programmatic bids. Targets: sustained 5k rps with burst to 15k rps; p50 < 5ms, p95 < 20ms, p99 < 50ms end-to-end for model decision call. Cost sensitivity: target cost per decision <$0.0006. Model preferences: open weights (Llama2-13B / Mistral-family) with fallback to smaller distilled models. Critical features: per-route batching with latency SLO overrides, prefix/KV caching to reduce repeated token costs, quantization (int8/int4) recommendations but must validate quality on Copperleaf holdout dataset. Security: Okta SSO (SAML), AWS KMS integration, SOC2 compliance, US data residency and audit logs retention for 12 months.

text: CRM notes: short-form, from AE/SE/Legal threads. PoC showed strong perf but borderline on p95 target — need caching + batching suggestions from Optimize team. CTO (Lars M.): 'can't accept maintenance during our CET peak; uptime must be effectively 99.99 for checkout flows.' Procurement: pushing for explicit financial credits and termination rights if SLO repeatedly missed. Sales: pushed a reserved GPU plan (8 -> 12) with burst pricing; client wants firm guarantee for Black Friday. Engineering asked for clarification on what counts as downtime (network glitches vs degraded performance). Legal flagged indemnity language and force majeure clause — Redwood to propose softened penalty cap. Action items: (1) finalize credit schedule with legal, (2) propose maintenance windows tailored to Cobalt's traffic calendar, (3) present revised reserved-GPU pricing matrix, (4) get Optimize recommendat...

preview:

text:
Summary: enterprise support SaaS provider (customer-facing ticketing + bot) looking to replace aging in-house assist and improve RAG fidelity. Primary driver: reduce time-to-resolution for tier 1 by 30% and reduce agent ramp time via high-quality assist suggestions with provenance.

Key pain: hallucinations during escalations; stale KB answers; connectors to Zendesk/Confluence are brittle; spikes during product launches make current system drop suggestions (time-outs). Wants citations-first UX: show doc snippet + link, then suggested action.

Quote from VP Support on 2026-02-14 call: "If the model can't cite a doc we can't send that to an agent in a live escalation — provenance is non-negotiable."

Technical requirements (customer language):
- Ingest connectors: Zendesk, Confluence, S3 buckets, Postgres changelogs, product release notes feed (RSS)
- Freshness target: <= 24h for releas...

title: Pebbleline ContactFlux
preview: Top-line: Pebbleline runs a global CCaaS platform for 18k enterprise seats. Looking to add streaming agent-assist (inline suggestions + canned responses) and automatic ticket summarization into their agent desktop. POC goal: demonstrate sub-150ms p95 token latency for streaming suggestions, deterministic tool-callin...
text:
Top-line: Pebbleline runs a global CCaaS platform for 18k enterprise seats. Looking to add streaming agent-assist (inline suggestions + canned responses) and automatic ticket summarization into their agent desktop. POC goal: demonstrate sub-150ms p95 token latency for streaming suggestions, deterministic tool-calling to their ticketing system, and graceful degradation under 3x baseline traffic (simulate Black Friday).

Key technical asks / quotes: "We need suggestions to feel instantaneous — anything over 200ms is disruptive to agent flow." Wants explicit fallback policy: small local quantized model for bursts, Redwood routing to regional capacity, and audit trail for all generated suggestions.

Integration notes: front-end is React-based agent desktop that connects to their telephony layer via websockets. Tool-calls currently go to Jira and an internal dispatch API; must support an i...

title: Cedarbridge Security AI
preview: CRM notes - Cedarbridge (FS, EU HQ). Primary ask: Private VPC deployment with customer-managed KMS and strict data residency in eu-west-1. They run AML/risk-monitoring workloads (embeddings + reranking) where data cannot leave EU. Key items discussed: - SIG/CAIQ: Cedarbridge sent partial CAIQ. They expect completed ...
text: CRM notes - Cedarbridge (FS, EU HQ). Primary ask: Private VPC deployment with customer-managed KMS and strict data residency in eu-west-1. They run AML/risk-monitoring workloads (embeddings + reranking) where data cannot leave EU. Key items discussed: - SIG/CAIQ: Cedarbridge sent partial CAIQ. They expect completed CAIQ back plus clarifications on a handful of control responses (data deletion, third-party subprocessors). - Shared responsibility: they require explicit matrix stating Redwood handles runtime, OS patching, model weights, but customer controls keys, network, and ingress policies. We drafted matrix and will formalize during security deep-dive. - KMS integration: customer-managed CMK in AWS KMS preferred. Need to validate key policies and cross-account grants; SE to provide example IAM policies. - Audit logging: retention 1 year minimum, logs exported to customer S3 account ...
